# Code of Conduct

Be the kind of person other people want to ship infrastructure with.

We work on audit-grade tools used in regulated industries. The discipline
of the code reflects the discipline of the community. We expect:

- **Respect the work.** Read before commenting. Run before reviewing.
  The substrate is technical; arguments are welcome; ad hominem is not.
- **Respect the contributors.** Most people contributing here are doing
  it on their own time, often around a job and a life. Patience is the
  default tone.
- **Receipts over rhetoric.** If you claim a thing — a bug, a flaw, a
  vulnerability, a regression — show the receipts. Tool output, repro
  steps, a failing test. The same discipline we hold our adapters to.
- **No personal attacks, no harassment, no discrimination.** On race,
  gender, sexual orientation, religion, disability, nationality, or
  anything else. Disagreement on technical merit is fine; disagreement
  on someone's right to be in the room is not.
- **Disclose AI assistance.** Use AI tools freely, but disclose them in
  PR descriptions when the AI did substantial work. The audit-grade
  discipline applies to us too.

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies to all project spaces — GitHub issues, PRs,
discussions, the Discord, email correspondence about the project, and
any in-person events where the project is being represented.

## Enforcement

Reports go to **isaacsight@gmail.com**. We'll triage within 48 hours and
respond with one of: dismissal (the report was unfounded), private
mediation, a public warning, or a ban from the project spaces. We won't
explain our reasoning publicly unless asked; we will explain it
privately to the reporter.

## Inspired by, not bound by

This Code of Conduct draws on the spirit of the
[Contributor Covenant 2.1](https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/1/code_of_conduct/)
but is intentionally shorter. Long codes of conduct produce
hair-splitting; short ones produce judgment. We prefer the second.
